authoritySource: Dr. Ahmed Hesham — OET Reading Comprehensive Rulebook (AI Training Edition). Encodes every CANDIDATE-FACING exam-UX rule from the PDF as content-as-code. The profession is 'medicine' as a sentinel because R01.6 makes Reading profession-agnostic — the same exam-mode rules apply to every candidate. The companion authoring rulebook lives at rulebooks/reading/{profession}/rulebook.v1.json.

sections.title: Parts B & C — Marking & Interaction Rules

rules.title: Reading is sub-test 2
rules.body: Reading is the second sub-test in the OET exam. The sequence is: Listening → Reading → Writing → Speaking.

rules.title: 42 questions: 20 / 6 / 16
rules.body: Total questions: 42. Three parts: Part A (20 questions / 20 marks), Part B (6 questions / 6 marks), Part C (16 questions / 16 marks).

rules.title: Part A 15 min; B+C 45 min combined
rules.body: Part A is a standalone section with its own 15-minute time block. Part B and Part C are delivered together in a shared 45-minute block.

rules.title: Total Reading duration 60 min
rules.body: Total Reading exam duration: 60 minutes (15 min Part A + 45 min Parts B and C).

rules.title: Part C split C1/C2 (8/8)
rules.body: Part C is divided into C1 (8 questions) and C2 (8 questions).

rules.title: Profession-agnostic format
rules.body: Reading applies the same format and content for ALL OET professions.

rules.title: 30/42 = Grade B (pass)
rules.body: 30 correct answers out of 42 = Grade B = 350 points = minimum passing score for doctors (GMC, ECFMG) and nurses (NMC).

rules.title: Scoring scale
rules.body: Scoring scale: Grade A = 450+, Grade B = 350–449, Grade C+ = 300–349, Grade C = 250–299, Grade D = below 250.

rules.title: 1 mark per correct, no negative
rules.body: Each correct answer = 1 mark. No negative marking for wrong answers.

rules.title: No score clipping for Medicine
rules.body: No combining (clipping) of scores across exam sittings for Medicine pathways (GMC, ECFMG, Canada). All four sub-tests must be passed in one sitting.

rules.title: Nursing (NMC) score combining
rules.body: Some nursing pathways (NMC) permit combining sub-test scores across sittings. This does not apply to Medicine.

rules.title: Part A = 4 texts on one topic
rules.body: Part A contains 4 texts: Text A, Text B, Text C, Text D. All 4 texts relate to a single clinical or healthcare topic.

rules.title: Part A question types
rules.body: Part A has three question types across 20 questions: Q1–7 Matching (write A/B/C/D); Q8–14 Short answer from text; Q15–20 Sentence completion (single missing word/number).

rules.title: Text C usually has a table/graph
rules.body: Text C almost always contains a table, graph, or chart. Recent exams have also placed tables in Texts A, B, or D.

rules.title: Part A answers come from the text
rules.body: Part A is a note-completion and short-answer section. Answers must be sourced directly from the text — they are not opinion-based.

rules.title: Part A 15 min; split layout in CBT
rules.body: Part A timing: 15 minutes. In the paper-based exam, the text booklet and answer sheet are separate. In the computer-based exam, text appears on the left panel and questions on the right panel.

rules.title: After 15 min: Part A locks/collects
rules.body: After 15 minutes: in the paper-based exam the invigilator collects Part A papers. In the computer-based exam the text panel locks automatically. No further access to Part A is possible.

rules.title: Spelling error = whole word wrong
rules.body: Spelling error in a Part A answer: any incorrect letter in a word = the entire word is marked wrong. The answer is expected to be copied directly from the text.

rules.title: Singular/plural error = wrong
rules.body: Singular/plural error: writing the wrong number form (singular instead of plural, or vice versa) = answer marked wrong.

rules.title: Wrong form (range vs max) = wrong
rules.body: Incorrect form of the answer: writing a range when the question asks for a maximum, or writing a dose per kg when the question asks for a total dose, = wrong answer.

rules.title: Numbers verbatim with units
rules.body: Numbers must be written exactly as they appear in the text — correct units, decimal notation, and formatting are required.

rules.title: Reading stricter than Listening
rules.body: Reading Part A has stricter spelling rules than Listening Part A. In Reading, any spelling error results in the answer being marked wrong with no exceptions.

rules.title: Q8–14 / Q15–20 word-for-word
rules.body: Answers for Q8–14 and Q15–20 must come word-for-word from the text. Paraphrasing or synonyms are not accepted.

rules.title: Part A 15-min hard limit
rules.body: Part A has a strict 15-minute time limit. When time expires, writing stops (paper-based) or the screen locks (computer-based).

rules.title: CBT auto-advance after 15 min
rules.body: In the computer-based exam: when the 15-minute timer reaches zero, the Part A text panel locks and the candidate proceeds automatically to the optional break screen before Part B and C.

rules.title: Paper: 'pencils down' at 15 min
rules.body: In the paper-based exam: the invigilator announces 'Pencils down' at 15 minutes and collects the Part A answer sheets and text booklets. No further writing is permitted.

rules.title: Paper: Part A sheet separate from B+C
rules.body: Part A answer sheet is separate from the Part B and C question booklet in the paper-based exam. Collecting Part A papers does not affect access to Parts B and C.

rules.title: Part B = 6 independent short texts
rules.body: Part B consists of 6 questions. Each question has its own short, independent text.

rules.title: Part B text and Q on same page
rules.body: Part B texts are short passages. Each text and its corresponding question appear on the same page.

rules.title: Part B = 3 options, exactly one correct
rules.body: Each Part B question has 3 answer choices (A, B, C). Exactly one is correct.

rules.title: Part B = 6 marks
rules.body: Part B carries 6 marks (1 mark per question).

rules.title: Parts B + C share 45-min block
rules.body: Part B and Part C share the same 45-minute time block and are contained in the same booklet.

rules.title: Part C = 2 long texts, 8 Q each
rules.body: Part C consists of 2 long texts: C1 and C2. Each carries 8 questions. Total: 16 questions / 16 marks.

rules.title: Part C text ~7–8 paragraphs
rules.body: Each Part C text is approximately 7–8 paragraphs long.

rules.title: Part C = 4 options, exactly one correct
rules.body: Each Part C question has 4 answer choices (A, B, C, D). Exactly one is correct.

rules.title: Part B = 3 choices; Part C = 4 choices
rules.body: Part B has 3 choices; Part C has 4 choices. This distinction is important for the AI to recognise correctly.

rules.title: C1 vs C2: unrelated topics
rules.body: C1 and C2 are on completely different topics with no connection to each other.

rules.title: Part C questions follow paragraph order
rules.body: Part C questions follow paragraph order within each text. Question 1 of C1 corresponds to the first paragraph; question 8 to the last. Questions do not reverse direction.

rules.title: Underlined paragraph → ~2 questions
rules.body: If a paragraph contains an underlined word or sentence, approximately 2 questions from that paragraph are expected.

rules.title: 4–5 Part C qs are inference / attitude
rules.body: Approximately 4–5 questions per Part C text are based on the writer's point of view or implied meaning, rather than explicitly stated facts. Two answer choices may both appear plausible; the task is to identify the more correct one.

rules.title: Remaining Part C answers are explicit
rules.body: The remaining 11–12 questions per Part C text have answers directly and explicitly stated in the text.

rules.title: B+C 45 min: candidate-pacing
rules.body: Part C and Part B share the same 45-minute block. Time allocation within this block is not enforced by the exam — it is entirely the candidate's responsibility.

rules.title: B/C: 1 mark each, no negative
rules.body: Each question in Parts B and C = 1 mark. There is no negative marking.

rules.title: B/C: one answer per question
rules.body: Only one answer choice can be selected per question. Selecting more than one is not permitted.

rules.title: Blank answer = 0 marks (no auto-fill)
rules.body: An unanswered question = 0 marks. The system does not auto-fill blank answers.

rules.title: CBT: left-click selects, right-click strikes
rules.body: In the computer-based exam: left-clicking an answer choice selects it (highlighted in selection colour). Right-clicking applies a strikethrough (elimination) without selecting.

rules.title: Strikethrough ≠ selection
rules.body: Strikethrough / elimination in the computer-based exam does NOT count as a selected answer. The candidate must still left-click a final answer.

rules.title: Paper: fill answer circle clearly
rules.body: In the paper-based exam: candidates fill in the answer circle (A, B, C, or D) on the answer sheet. Partial fills or ambiguous marks may be rejected.

rules.title: CBT: no break Listening → Reading
rules.body: No break between the Listening and Reading sub-tests in the computer-based exam. Reading begins automatically after Listening ends.

rules.title: 10-min optional break after Part A
rules.body: Between Part A and Parts B+C in the computer-based exam: a 10-minute optional break is offered. The candidate may press 'Resume Test' to skip it and proceed immediately.

rules.title: After 15 min Part A: permanent lock
rules.body: After the 15-minute Part A period: the Part A text panel locks permanently. No access to Part A content or answers is possible after this point.

rules.title: B+C 45 min: sequential navigation
rules.body: Within the 45-minute Parts B+C block: the candidate moves through questions sequentially. Navigation rules vary by delivery mode (see below).

rules.title: CBT B+C: Next + confirm at boundaries
rules.body: In the computer-based exam, the candidate moves forward through questions by pressing Next. Confirmation is required at certain section boundaries.

rules.title: End of B+C: screen locks, exam ends
rules.body: At the end of the 45-minute Parts B+C block in the computer-based exam: the screen locks and the Reading sub-test ends. No further answers can be submitted.

rules.title: Part A: text left, questions right
rules.body: Part A display: the text appears on the LEFT panel of the screen. The questions and answer boxes appear on the RIGHT panel — a split-screen layout.

rules.title: B/C: text + question on same screen
rules.body: Parts B and C display: each short Part B text appears on the same screen as its question. Each Part C long text appears alongside the corresponding questions on the same screen.

rules.title: Timer always visible
rules.body: The timer is always visible on screen throughout the computer-based exam.

rules.title: Part A: highlighting is available
rules.body: Part A — Highlighting: available and functional. Candidates may highlight text in the passage during Part A.

rules.title: Part A copy-paste is unreliable
rules.body: Part A — Copy-paste: not always available. It is unreliable and varies by exam centre. It cannot be depended upon.

rules.title: B/C: passage highlighting permitted
rules.body: Parts B and C — Highlighting the text passage: permitted.

rules.title: B/C: question-stem highlighting permitted
rules.body: Parts B and C — Highlighting the question stem: permitted.

rules.title: B/C: choices CANNOT be highlighted
rules.body: Parts B and C — Highlighting the answer choices (A/B/C/D): NOT permitted.

rules.title: B/C: right-click strikethrough
rules.body: Parts B and C — Eliminating an answer choice: right-click applies a strikethrough to a choice without selecting it.

rules.title: Zoom: app +/− buttons, Ctrl+ blocked
rules.body: Zoom: + and − buttons within the exam application control zoom. The browser keyboard shortcut Ctrl+ is blocked in the exam environment.

rules.title: CBT: no margin annotations
rules.body: In the computer-based exam, candidates cannot write paragraph numbers in the margins or make physical annotations.

rules.title: Min screen resolution 1920×1080
rules.body: Minimum screen resolution: 1920 × 1080. Below this, the split-screen layout fails — text and questions overlap or are cut off.

rules.title: Display scale ≤ 125%
rules.body: Display scale (Windows): 100% or maximum 125%. Scale above 125% causes interface panels to overlap and clip.

rules.title: At-home: pre-exam display config required
rules.body: At-home candidates: must configure screen resolution and display scale before exam day. Failure has caused exam cancellations.

rules.title: ProProctor dedicated app
rules.body: The exam runs inside a dedicated application (ProProctor), not a regular browser.

rules.title: Stable broadband required
rules.body: Internet connection: must be stable throughout the exam. An unstable connection can interrupt the exam session.

rules.title: VPN / VM must be off
rules.body: All VPNs and virtual machines must be disabled before launching the exam application.

rules.title: Test-centre auto-configured display
rules.body: At test centres: split screen and display settings are pre-configured. No candidate action is required. This applies to at-home candidates only.

rules.title: Paper Listening → Reading: ~10-min gap
rules.body: In the paper-based exam, there is a natural 10-minute transition between Listening and Reading while papers are collected and new papers are distributed.

rules.title: Paper Part A: text booklet + answer sheet
rules.body: Part A: two separate documents are distributed — the text booklet (Texts A, B, C, D) and the answer sheet.

rules.title: Paper: invigilator collects Part A at 15 min
rules.body: After 15 minutes: the invigilator collects both the Part A text booklet and answer sheet. Access to Part A is permanently over.

rules.title: Paper B+C in single combined booklet
rules.body: Parts B and C are delivered in a single combined booklet. The text and the question appear together in the same document.

rules.title: Paper B+C: free navigation within 45 min
rules.body: In the paper-based exam, the candidate can view and write in any question across Parts B and C at any time within the 45-minute block. There are no section locks.

rules.title: Paper: pencil annotations everywhere
rules.body: In the paper-based exam, candidates may write paragraph numbers in the margins and annotate the question paper freely with a pencil.

rules.title: Paper: pencil highlight/underline allowed
rules.body: Highlighting and underlining in the paper-based exam: permitted in all parts using a pencil.

rules.title: Paper timer: wall projector / clock
rules.body: Timer in the paper-based exam: displayed on a wall projector or clock visible to all candidates. The invigilator announces time warnings.

rules.title: Paper Part C: text and choices may be on separate pages
rules.body: Part C paper-based format: the long text and the answer choices may be on different pages within the booklet, requiring the candidate to flip between them. This is a key structural difference from the computer-based exam where both are on the same screen.
